name: MS (mineral standard) minimal medium (PMC60676)
description: MS (mineral standard) minimal medium — medium reported in PMC60676 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC60676 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Base medium identified as 'MS (mineral standard) minimal medium' (Richaud C, Mengin-Lecreulx D, Pochet S, Johnson EJ, Cohen GN, Marliere P. Directed evolution of biosynthetic pathways. Recruitment of cysteine thioethers for constructing the cell wall of Escherichia coli. J Biol Chem. 1993;268(36):26827-26835. PMID 8262915; DOI 10.1016/S0021-9258(19)74186-6. (Cited as reference [24] in PMC60676 as the definition of MS medium.)) but its full composition could not be recovered from openly-available
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.formulation_warning: promises minerals, none present; fewer than 3 real components
provenance.verification_note: Paper = 'Long term adaptation... overcoming thymineless death by experimental evolution of E. coli' (PMC60676), organism E. coli. The paper does NOT give the full MS (mineral standard) formulation — it defers to cited reference 24 (Richaud et al. 1993), which I could not access. Only verifiable facts from THIS paper: carbon source D-glucose 10 mM (= 2 g/L), thymine 1 µM, and AEROBIC growth (bubble
provenance.base_medium: MS (mineral standard) minimal medium of the Marliere laboratory, defined in Richaud et al. 1993 J Biol Chem 268:26827-26835 (cited as reference [24] in PMC60676).
provenance.base_medium_reference: Richaud C, Mengin-Lecreulx D, Pochet S, Johnson EJ, Cohen GN, Marliere P. Directed evolution of biosynthetic pathways. Recruitment of cysteine thioethers for constructing the cell wall of Escherichia coli. J Biol Chem. 1993;268(36):26827-26835. PMID 8262915; DOI 10.1016/S0021-9258(19)74186-6. (Cited as reference [24] in PMC60676 as the definition of MS medium.)
provenance.verification_evidence: Bacteria were routinely grown in mineral standard (MS) medium [24] containing 2 g glucose/liter with or without thymine (1 to 10 uM) or in LB medium [25].

sources_chased.how: WebFetch pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC60676 and EBI europepmc fullTextXML
added: confirmation that the MS mineral base is not spelled out here and is deferred to ref [24] Richaud 1993

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as ref [24] = Richaud C, Mengin-Lecreulx D, Pochet S, Johnson EJ, Cohen GN, Marliere P. J Biol Chem 1993;268:26827-26835 (DOI 10.1016/S0021-9258(19)74186-6; PMID 8262915) -- the actual definition of MS medium (all salts, phosphate buffer, ammonium N-source, Mg, trace metals, thiamine)
sources_chased.how: Attempted DOI resolver, JBC /fulltext and /pdf, ScienceDirect article + /pdfft, legacy intl.jbc.org, and Wayback Machine. Unpaywall reports is_oa=true (JBC hybrid-OA) but the only location is the DOI, and every host returns HTTP 403 / Cloudflare-Elsevier bot-challenge HTML (not the PDF). Article is not in PMC/EuropePMC (no PMCID, inEPMC=N) and is not in the PMC OA subset, so no XML/PDF is retrievable. No full-text Wayback snapshot exists.

sources_chased.what: Same-lab reproductions that might restate the MS recipe: Science 2001 (Doring/Marliere valine, 10.1126/science.1057718), Appl Microbiol Biotechnol 2007 continuous-culture (10.1007/s00253-007-1168-5), Angew Chem 2011 'Chemical evolution of a bacterium's genome' (10.1002/anie.201100535), J Bacteriol 1994 lanthionine paper (PMC205645 / 10.1128/jb.176.14.4321-4327.1994), Genetics 1998 cysteine-reassignment (PMC1460352)
sources_chased.how: Science 2001 and AMB 2007 paywalled (Unpaywall is_oa=false, Springer/Science 403). Angew 2011 OA copy resolves to a KU Leuven Lirias JS/PDF.js SPA handle page with no direct bitstream. PMC205645 and PMC1460352 render only abstract+references (old scanned articles; PMC205645 flagged 'not Open Access' by the PMC OA API, so no Methods text/PDF). None yielded a verbatim MS-medium composition.

recuration.notes: The paper (E. coli, facultative but cultivated aerobically) does not spell out the MS 'mineral standard' base; it only states the carbon source (glucose 2 g/L routine, 10 mM in the evolution and growth-rate experiments), the thymine supplement (1-10 uM, required by the thymine-auxotroph strain and withdrawn to impose thymineless death), and 37 C. The complete mineral base -- phosphate buffer, ammonium (N source), Mg, trace metals (Fe etc.), and thiamine -- is a defined recipe that lives entirely in cited reference [24] (Richaud et al. 1993, J Biol Chem), which could not be obtained: it is OA-h

components.name: Thymine
components.role: pyrimidine growth factor (required by the thyA-deficient / thymine-requiring strain; withdrawn to impose thymine starvation)

oxygen_note: no oxygen requirement specified; O2 available but not asserted
